risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The allocation of the participants to the study arms was done centrally by block randomization (block size of 6) stratified by country... The Pharmacy Department of the Hospital Clinic in Barcelona produced and safeguarded the computer-generated randomization list for each recruiting site until unblinding" Comment: This describes a centrally administered computer-generated randomization list with block randomization and stratification, which is sufficient to indicate a proper random allocation sequence.

_evidence.method_quote: The allocation of the participants to the study arms was done centrally by block randomization (block size of 6) stratified by country to receive CTX (Septrin, UCB Pharma, fixed combination 800 mg of trimethroprim and 160 mg of sulfamethoxazole/tablet) plus IPTp-MQ (Lariam, Roche, 250 mg of MQ base/tablet) or CTX plus IPTp-placebo (identical to MQ tablets in shape and colour). The Pharmacy Department of the Hospital Clinic in Barcelona produced and safeguarded the computer-generated randomization list for each recruiting site until unblinding, and carried out the masking, labelling, and packaging of all study interventional drugs.
_evidence.method_summary: Central computer-generated block randomization (block size 6), stratified by country.

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Study number allocation for each participant was concealed in opaque sealed envelopes that were sequentially numbered and opened only after recruitment by study health personnel." Comment: This explicitly describes a SNOSE mechanism (sequentially numbered, opaque, sealed envelopes) opened only after recruitment, which prevents foreknowledge of the upcoming assignment by the recruiter.

_evidence.concealment_quote: "Study number allocation for each participant was concealed in opaque sealed envelopes that were sequentially numbered and opened only after recruitment by study health personnel."

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: “An individually randomized double-blind placebo-controlled trial was conducted” and “Investigators, laboratory staff, care providers, and study participants were blinded to intervention throughout the study.” Comment: The study explicitly states that both participants and care providers/personnel were blinded throughout the trial, using a placebo-controlled design with identical tablets and centralized masking/packaging, so knowledge of allocation was unlikely to affect outcomes.

_evidence.blinding_quote: “An individually randomized double-blind placebo-controlled trial was conducted” and “Investigators, laboratory staff, care providers, and study participants were blinded to intervention throughout the study.”
_evidence.blinding_method: placebo-controlled; CTXp plus IPTp-MQ or CTX plus IPTp-placebo (identical to MQ tablets in shape and colour); the Pharmacy Department “carried out the masking, labelling, and packaging of all study interventional drugs.”
outcome_categories: maternal and fetal parasitemia one month after delivery
objective_outcomes: maternal and fetal parasitemia one month after delivery

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Investigators, laboratory staff, care providers, and study participants were blinded to intervention throughout the study." Comment: Outcome assessment was explicitly blinded. The main outcome assessors included laboratory staff for smear/PCR, Hb, CD4, and viral load, and the clinical outcome data were collected under blinded study conditions. Although some outcomes were clinical or symptom-based, the paper states that investigators and laboratory staff were blinded throughout, which is sufficient to judge this domain low risk.

_evidence.assessor_blinding_quote: Investigators, laboratory staff, care providers, and study participants were blinded to intervention throughout the study.

outcomes.label: peripheral maternal malaria infection at delivery

risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "The main analysis was done on the Intention-to-Treat (ITT) cohort that included all recruited women that met the inclusion criteria and had data on the specific outcomes, and on the According to Protocol (ATP) cohort that included all women who had received three doses of IPTp according to the pre-specified schedule, and have results available on peripheral parasitemia at delivery." Comment: The article reports that the ITT primary outcome was based on women with outcome data, and it also reports an ATP/per-protocol-style cohort, but the supplied text does not fully specify per-arm post-randomization losses for every outcome/time point in a way that clearly establishes attrition bias for the outcomes of interest. The CONSORT figures are referenced, and the primary endpoint shows 490/537 vs 483/534 analysed, but the presence of an ATP analysis alone does not prove high risk; the observed missingness appears modest and fairly balanced. However, because the paper’s text does not give complete, directly interpretable attrition details for all analysed outcomes and the analysis includes a restricted ATP cohort, the safest Cochrane judgement here is unclear rather than high or low.

per_arm_flow.reasons: Figures 1 and S1 show the trial profiles for the ITT and the ATP cohorts. The main reasons for non-enrolment into the trial were baseline HIV test negative (52.5%), no permanent residence in the study area (24.5%), and gestational age >28 weeks (12.5%). The second IPTp dose was not given to 4.7% of women who had received the first dose of placebo; the third dose was not given to 7.5% of the women who had received the second dose of placebo.

Figures 1 and S1 show the trial profiles for the ITT and the ATP cohorts. The main reasons for non-enrolment into the trial were baseline HIV test negative (52.5%), no permanent residence in the study area (24.5%), and gestational age >28 weeks (12.5%). The second IPTp dose was not given to 7.8% of women who had received the first dose of MQ; the third dose was not given to 10.6% of the women who had received the second dose of MQ.

_evidence.missing_data_method: ITT and ATP; ITT included all recruited women that met the inclusion criteria and had data on the specific outcomes, ATP included all women who had received three doses of IPTp according to the pre-specified schedule, and have results available on peripheral parasitemia at delivery
_evidence.notes: Primary outcome at delivery had analysed numbers 490/537 control and 483/534 MQ (973 total). Total loss % computed from missing primary-outcome data: 47/537 (8.8%) and 51/534 (9.6%), average 9.0%. ATP analyses were reported for the primary endpoint, with per-protocol-style restriction to women receiving three doses.
